summary: Ukrainian air defenses shot down 79 hostile UAVs in a large-scale attack involving 90 Shahed‑type drones across northern, southern and eastern Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/65566). Ukrainian drones penetrated layered air defence over Moscow, striking the Kapotnya refinery for the second time this week (Source: @UkraineNow/82037). Russian attacks continued, killing an 8‑year‑old in Pavlograd and damaging civilian buses in Kherson, causing multiple injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82041, @UkraineNow/82046).
key_points: Ukrainian air defence downed 79 enemy UAVs, intercepting 90 Shahed‑type drones from multiple Russian directions (Source: @kpszsu/65566).
Ukrainian drones breached Moscow's layered air defence and hit the Kapotnya refinery, a key Russian military‑industrial target (Source: @UkraineNow/82037).
An 8‑year‑old girl was killed and a 49‑year‑old woman injured in a Russian attack on Pavlograd, with two private houses damaged (Source: @UkraineNow/82041).
Russian forces damaged two buses in Kherson's Korabelnyi district, wounding five civilians who were hospitalized with moderate injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82046).

Multiple Shahed and other UAVs were reported over Kharkiv, Poltava, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia regions, prompting local shelter alerts (Source: @kpszsu/65565, @kpszsu/65598, @kpszsu/65589, @kpszsu/65596, @kpszsu/65583).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the north‑east front, with warnings of possible air‑to‑ground attacks (Source: @kpszsu/65587).
Strategic bomber relocations were reported, including four Tu‑22M3 moving from Olenya to Engels (Source: @eRadarrua/97256).
A Russian drone strike on civilian vessels in the Black Sea killed one crew member and injured several others (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defences downed 79 hostile UAVs in a massive raid involving 90 Shahed‑type drones across multiple fronts (Source: @kpszsu/65566). Ukrainian drones breached Moscow's air defence and struck the Kapotnya refinery, marking a significant blow to Russian military infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82037). Russian attacks continued to cause civilian casualties, including the death of an 8‑year‑old in Pavlograd and injuries from bus attacks in Kherson (Source: @UkraineNow/82041, @UkraineNow/82046).

points: Ukrainian forces reported numerous UAV sightings: Shahed and other drones over Kharkiv from the north, over Poltava heading to Hrebinka, and over Sumy heading to Okhtyrka (Source: @kpszsu/65565).
Ukrainian drones were observed moving from the Black Sea towards Odesa/Saratov direction (Source: @kpszsu/65583).
Ukrainian UAVs were detected in Dnipropetrovsk heading to Petrykivka and in Zaporizhzhia targeting Nizhna Khortytsia (Source: @kpszsu/65582, @eRadarrua/97286).
Ukrainian tactical aviation launched guided bomb strikes in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/65597).
Ukrainian air defence intercepted 79 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and Bandera types, across the country (Source: @kpszsu/65566).
Ukrainian drones successfully penetrated Moscow's air defence, striking the Kapotnya refinery for the second time this week (Source: @UkraineNow/82037).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed drone crossing from Chernihiv to Kyiv/Boryspil corridor (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67902).
Russian forces conducted a drone strike on civilian vessels in the Black Sea, killing one crew member and wounding several others (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
A Russian attack on Pavlograd resulted in the death of an 8‑year‑old girl and injuries to a 49‑year‑old woman, with two private houses destroyed (Source: @UkraineNow/82041).
Russian troops attacked public transport in Kherson, damaging two buses and injuring five civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82046).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑east front, with warnings of possible air‑to‑ground strikes (Source: @kpszsu/65587).
Russian strategic bombers were relocated: four Tu‑22M3 from Olenya to Engels, and two Tu‑95MS from the Far East to Engels (Source: @eRadarrua/97256).
Russian forces struck a fuel tanker parking lot in southern Odesa region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31017).
Russian authorities reported new biolab activity related to dangerous pathogens in Ukraine (Source: @tass_agency/381496).
UAVs were reported over Kharkiv from the north and heading towards Slobozhanske, indicating active aerial activity in the eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/65565).
Shahed and other attack drones were launched from directions including Bryansk and Orel, impacting northern, southern and eastern Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/65566).
Ukrainian air defence downed multiple hostile UAVs across the eastern front as part of the 79‑drone interception (Source: @kpszsu/65566).
Russian tactical aviation warnings were issued for the north‑east direction, suggesting potential strikes on eastern front positions (Source: @kpszsu/65587).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed moving from the Black Sea towards Odesa/Saratov, potentially affecting eastern coastal areas (Source: @kpszsu/65583).
Strategic bomber movements to Engels may support future eastern operations (Source: @eRadarrua/97256).
UAV activity reported in Sumy heading towards Putyvl from the north and in Chernihiv near Bobrovytsia (Source: @kpszsu/65589, @eRadarrua/97233).
Shahed drones launched from Bryansk and Orel directions targeted northern Ukraine, part of the 90‑drone raid (Source: @kpszsu/65566).
Ukrainian air defence intercepted numerous hostile UAVs over the north, contributing to the 79‑drone tally (Source: @kpszsu/65566).
Russian tactical aviation activity warned for north‑east front, increasing threat to northern positions (Source: @kpszsu/65587).
Ukrainian UAV observed in north‑west Kyiv region heading south‑west, indicating continued aerial surveillance (Source: @kpszsu/65579).
Strategic bomber relocations may affect northern air operations (Source: @eRadarrua/97256).
UAVs reported over Kherson's Korabelnyi district damaging two buses and injuring five civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82046).
Russian drone strike on a civilian vessel in the Black Sea resulted in casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
Ukrainian drones penetrated Moscow's air defence and struck the Kapotnya refinery, a southern target relative to the capital (Source: @UkraineNow/82037).
Shahed drones launched from Gvardeyskoye (Crimea) contributed to the large‑scale attack (Source: @kpszsu/65566).
Ukrainian UAVs observed heading towards Petrykivka in Dnipropetrovsk, a southern‑central area (Source: @kpszsu/65582).
Russian forces struck a fuel tanker parking lot in southern Odesa region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31017).
Ukrainian air defence shot down 79 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and Bandera types, across multiple fronts (Source: @kpszsu/65566).
Ukrainian drones breached Moscow's layered air defence and hit the Kapotnya refinery for the second time this week (Source: @UkraineNow/82037).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the north‑east front, with warnings of possible air‑to‑ground attacks (Source: @kpszsu/65587).
Strategic bombers (Tu‑22M3 and Tu‑95MS) were relocated to Engels, indicating potential shifts in long‑range strike capabilities (Source: @eRadarrua/97256).
Russian drone attacks on civilian vessels in the Black Sea caused fatalities and injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
Ukrainian UAV sightings across Kharkiv, Poltava, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia suggest sustained low‑altitude aerial operations (Source: @kpszsu/65565, @kpszsu/65582).
Russian Shahed and other UAV launches from multiple directions (Bryansk, Orel, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, Gvardeyskoye) formed part of the 90‑drone offensive (Source: @kpszsu/65566).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defence downed 79 hostile UAVs in a coordinated response to a 90‑drone raid from multiple Russian directions, demonstrating robust anti‑air capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/65566).
Ukrainian drones successfully penetrated Moscow's air defence, striking the Kapotnya refinery, a critical Russian military‑industrial asset (Source: @UkraineNow/82037).
Russian attacks killed an 8‑year‑old in Pavlograd and injured civilians, highlighting ongoing threats to populated areas (Source: @UkraineNow/82041).
Russian drone strike on civilian buses in Kherson injured five people, showing continued targeting of transport infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82046).
Strategic bomber relocations (four Tu‑22M3 and three Tu‑95MS to Engels) suggest preparation for future long‑range operations (Source: @eRadarrua/97256).
Russian drone attacks on Black Sea vessels caused one death and multiple injuries, extending the conflict to maritime domains (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
Ukrainian UAV activity reported across multiple oblasts (Kharkiv, Poltava, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia) indicates sustained aerial reconnaissance and strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/65565).
sections.threat_assessment: The large‑scale UAV attack and the successful strike on Moscow's Kapotnya refinery demonstrate heightened aerial contestation, with both sides employing increasingly sophisticated drone tactics. Russian tactical aviation warnings and strategic bomber relocations point to potential escalation of air strikes on Ukrainian frontlines. Civilian targets continue to suffer from Russian drone and missile attacks, raising the risk of further casualties and infrastructure damage.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have issued shelter alerts across multiple regions and mobilised air defence assets, successfully intercepting a high number of hostile UAVs. Russian officials have emphasized the use of tactical aviation on the north‑east front and have continued strikes on civilian infrastructure, indicating a resolve to maintain pressure despite losses. International observers note the significance of the Moscow refinery strike as a symbolic and operational blow to Russian capabilities.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows a pattern of attacks originating from Bryansk, Orel, Primorsko‑Akhtarsk and Crimea, suggesting coordinated launch sites. The relocation of Tu‑22M3 and Tu‑95MS bombers to Engels may precede increased long‑range strike activity. Continued civilian targeting in Pavlograd and Kherson underscores the need for enhanced protective measures for urban populations.
